warmup.name: Wall neck stretch
warmup.why: Releases the neck before overhead and inverted work

warmup.name: Wall angels
warmup.why: Opens the shoulders and grooves overhead position

warmup.name: Shin raises (back against wall)
warmup.why: Wakes tibialis for landings and shin health

warmup.name: Arch curls (foot doming)
warmup.why: Wakes the foot arch — plantar fascia's support crew

warmup.name: Calf raise (slow eccentric)
warmup.why: Slow loading primes the fascia — different job than your jumping

warmup.name: Ulnar nerve glides
warmup.why: Cubital tunnel care — glide, never stretch to pain

warmup.name: Arm circles (small→large, both ways)
warmup.why: Blood into the shoulders through full range

warmup.name: Shoulder lifts
warmup.why: Wakes the shoulders before overhead and inverted work

warmup.name: Standing calf stretch
warmup.why: Lengthens the calf before impact work

warmup.name: Knee clocks
warmup.why: Knee tracking control through full circles

warmup.name: Wrist stretch
warmup.why: Preps the wrists for any hand-loading

warmup.name: Wrist lifts
warmup.why: Wakes the forearms and wrist extensors

warmup.name: Curl-ups
warmup.why: Switches on the deep core before hollow work

warmup.name: Leg lifts
warmup.why: Lower-ab activation before the main core block

warmup.name: Curl + leg lift combo
warmup.why: Ties upper and lower core together before hollow work

moves.name: Handstand wall hold
moves.why: Shoulder stack and balance (soft elbows, no hard lockout)

moves.name: Jump tucks
moves.why: B days only — plyo power without extra joint cost

moves.name: Hip abduction (band walks or side-lying)
moves.why: Glute med — landing control and knee tracking

moves.name: Bridge push-ups
moves.why: Bridge strength for walkovers and handsprings

moves.name: Single-leg glute bridge
moves.why: One-leg hip drive, zero impact

moves.name: Wall handstand shoulder taps
moves.why: Handstand stability under shift

moves.name: Candlestick to stand
moves.why: Rebound and landing mechanics

moves.name: Broad jump stick landing
moves.why: Quiet, absorbed landings — joint-friendly power

moves.name: Arch rocks
moves.why: Dynamic arch — the lower-back partner to hollow rocks

moves.name: Cossack squat
moves.why: Adductor and hamstring range under load — strength at the end range splits need

moves.name: Hollow rocks
moves.why: Staple A — hollow under momentum (1 rep = one full back-and-forth)

moves.name: Tuck-ups (fast)
moves.why: Staple B — explosive knee snap

moves.name: Hollow body hold
moves.why: Every-session staple — the shape behind every skill

moves.name: V-ups
moves.why: Fast hip flexion for rotation speed

moves.name: Side plank hip dips
moves.why: Oblique strength for sideways rotation

moves.name: Arch (superman) hold
moves.why: Back tension for handsprings

moves.name: L-sit or tuck sit hold
moves.why: Compression strength

moves.name: Single-leg V-up
moves.why: One-leg drive like the aerial kick

moves.name: Straight jump to fast tuck
moves.why: Set + tuck timing

moves.name: Dead bug
moves.why: Core stability with moving limbs

moves.name: Heel walks
moves.why: Toes-up walking — direct tibialis work for shin durability

moves.name: Bent-knee (soleus) calf raises
moves.why: The soleus absorbs landing impact — the untrained half of shin health

moves.name: Toe walks
moves.why: Ankle stiffness for rebound — pairs with heel walks

moves.name: Prone Y-T-W raises
moves.why: The home pull — lower traps and rhomboids that superman holds don't touch

moves.name: Copenhagen plank
moves.why: Adductor strength for the aerial's one-leg drive — end-range support for splits

moves.name: Nordic hamstring curl
moves.why: Home hamstring — eccentric strength that protects knees on landings

moves.name: Reverse Nordic curl
moves.why: Lengthened quad + patellar-tendon capacity for repeated landings

moves.name: Lateral bound (stick landing)
moves.why: Sideways power and one-leg landing control — the plane the aerial travels in

moves.name: Single-leg RDL
moves.why: One-leg hinge and balance — the takeoff-leg partner to Bulgarian split squats

moves.name: Hex bar deadlift
moves.why: Tumbling power; loads feet in a fascia-friendly way

moves.name: Overhead press
moves.why: Neutral wrists, don't slam elbow lockout (cubital tunnel)

moves.name: Face pulls or band pull-aparts
moves.why: Posture — balances all the pressing

moves.name: Pallof press
moves.why: Anti-rotation — the missing core pattern; steadies twisting takeoffs and landings

moves.name: Bulgarian split squat
moves.why: Single-leg drive and landing strength

moves.name: Chest-supported or cable row
moves.why: The missing pull your posture needs

moves.name: Leg press (light-moderate)
moves.why: Optional volume, easy on joints

moves.name: Leg curl
moves.why: Hamstrings protect knees on landings

moves.name: Leg extension
moves.why: Quad and knee-tendon capacity for jumping

moves.name: Tibialis raises
moves.why: Balances all that calf and jump work — shin and foot health

cooldown.name: Splits routine (incl. sciatic floss)
cooldown.why: Your routine — hip range plus the nerve work you already do

structure.dayAlternation: A on odd sessions, B on even
structure.selection: Each move carries goalScores (trainingGoalId -> 0..10). A move's base score = Σ over training goals of goal.weight × goalScore; moves scoring 0 are excluded. A recency boost favours moves not done recently. A move may carry a `tags` array of ids from the top-level `tags` list; each tag resolves to a 1–5 priority. Auto day tags (day-a, day-b) are a SOFT preference derived from the session's A/B day — today's day scores 4 (×1.5) and the other day 2 (×0.6), so a B-tagged move can still surface on an A day if it scores high enough (no hard lock). Every other tag reads settings.tagPriority (default 3). Priority 1 removes the move from the pool entirely; otherwise the base score is multiplied per tag: 2 → ×0.6, 3 → ×1, 4 → ×1.5, 5 → ×2.5. Moves are then picked greedily up to `moves`, with per-section diminishing returns (each already-picked move discounts its section) so Floor never crowds out Weights / Machines; the picks are grouped by section into Floor / Weights / Machines. A move flagged disabled is never picked. Superset bias (settings.supersetBias, 0–30) further nudges Floor moves that pair into supersets while Auto superset is on.
notes: Warm-up and cool-down are static and always cover the care goals (splits, plantar, cubital, posture, sciatic, recovery); only change them when the user explicitly asks

Training goals (flip, aerial, core, gym, shins) have 0–10 weight sliders in Settings and in the Gym tab's Adjust-session panel; weight 0 turns a goal off. Care goals are always on and have no sliders. The shins goal (tibialis + soleus durability) is served by heel/toe walks and bent-knee calf raises at home plus tibialis raises in the gym.

A move may carry a `tags` array of ids drawn from the top-level `tags` list. Each non-auto tag has a 1–5 priority slider in Settings (settings.tagPriority, default 3): 1 hard-avoids the move (drops it from the pool), 2 lowers its priority (×0.6), 3 is neutral, 4/5 raise it (×1.5 / ×2.5). The joint-stress-legs (knees/ankles) and joint-stress-arms (shoulders/elbows/wrists) tags let a recovering athlete spare a region. The gym-only tag marks every weights/machines move (equipment the user has only at the gym); set its priority to 1 to hard-exclude them for an at-home, bodyweight-on-a-mat session. Day A/B are auto tags (auto:"day"): they express a soft same-day preference (today's day ×1.5, the other ×0.6) rather than a hard lock, and never appear as sliders. Moves may also be individually disabled in the Moves tab (disabled:true).

Health constraints: cubital tunnel (no hard elbow lockout pressing), plantar fasciitis (keep slow calf loading, conservative impact), sciatic nerve (gentle flossing lives in user's splits routine), posture (never drop the pulling work), user stims by jumping (extra daily impact through feet)
